conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you a few questions. Let me start with:

**Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you asking about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child - my son Alex. He's 16 years old and lives with me. I'd like to know if I'm eligible to claim Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! I can see you have a 16-year-old son named Alex who lives with you.

To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to ask:

**Is Alex currently in approved education or training (such as school, college, or an approved training program)?**

This is important because children aged 16-19 can still qualify for Child Benefit if they're in approved education or training.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not in any education or training at the moment. He finished school recently and is just taking some time to figure out what he wants to do next. He's not studying anywhere right now.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. Let me ask one more important question to complete my assessment:

**Are you responsible for Alex - meaning do you live with him and are you the person who is primarily caring for him and meeting his needs?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. He lives here at home with me, and I'm the one looking after him - I provide for all his needs, pay for everything he needs, and he's completely dependent on me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. I now have enough details to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Based on the UK Child Benefit rules, I need to inform you of my assessment:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Unfortunately, **you are not currently eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**. 

Here's why: Child Benefit can be claimed for children under 16, or for young people aged 16-19 if they remain in approved education or training (such as A-levels, Scottish Highers, NVQs, or similar qualifications). Since Alex is 16 and is not currently enrolled in any approved education or training program, he doesn't meet the eligibility criteria.

**However**, if Alex decides to return to education or start an approved training program in the future, you would be able to claim Child Benefit for him again at that point, up until he turns 20 (or until he leaves approved education/training, whichever comes first).

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 16 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 16 and not in approved education or extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 16 and not in approved education or extension period
circumstances: Child is 16 and not in approved education or extension period
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 16 years old and not currently in approved education or training. According to UK Child Benefit rules, you can claim Child Benefit for children under 16, or for those aged 16-19 if they are in approved education or training. Since Alex has finished school and is not enrolled in any education or training program, he does not meet the age and education criteria for Child Benefit eligibility, even though he lives with you and you are responsible for him.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is responsible for one child, Alex, who is 16 years old and lives with them. However, Alex is not currently in approved education or training. Under Child Benefit rules, children aged 16-19 only qualify if they remain in approved education or training. Since Alex does not meet this requirement, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for him at this time.
